expectations.text: Two separate output files are created, one per source image, each named after its corresponding image
Each output file contains only the text from its own source image, with no cross-contamination between the two images' content
expectations.evidence: architecture-agentique.txt only contains the diagram's content; exemple-prompt-B.txt only contains the sub-agent prompt template content — no overlap

expectations.text: Neither output file contains a 'Summary' section (none was requested)
expectations.evidence: Both files end with transcribed content, no '---'/'Summary:' block in either

expectations.text: Both files are non-empty and reflect distinct content matching their respective source images
expectations.evidence: exemple-prompt-B.txt accurately reproduces '{ROLE_AGENT}', '(format strict)', and the Contraintes/Outils sections matching the source; architecture-agentique.txt matches the diagram content

claims.evidence: The output uses '·' and '◆' bullet glyphs that don't appear to be in the source image (which uses plain '-' bullets per visual inspection) — a minor decorative deviation, not a content error

suggestions.reason: No assertion catches the invented bullet glyphs ('·'/'◆' instead of the source's plain '-'). Minor, but a stricter 'preserves original formatting/bullet style' assertion would catch it.

eval_feedback.overall: Strong pass; only a cosmetic formatting deviation noted, no content accuracy issues.
